Quality Education.reason: Course promotes critical thinking and diverse observations on animal issues, linking to Target 4.7 ('knowledge for sustainable development').

Life on Land.reason: Course discusses animal ethics and conservation, linking to Target 15.5 ('reduce degradation of natural habitats').

Zero Hunger.reason: The course discusses "economic animals" and "pastoralists," which relates to food production systems and domesticated animals, linking to Targets 2.4 and 2.5.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course mentions "狂犬" (rabies), a communicable disease, in the context of human-animal interaction, which has a minor link to Target 3.3.
evidence: 狂犬、名犬、流浪犬 (rabies, famous dogs, stray dogs)

Quality Education.reason: The course aims for learners to acquire knowledge on human-animal interactions to "propose solutions," aligning with education for sustainable development as per Target 4.7.
evidence: 反省現狀並試圖提出解決方向 (reflect on the current situation and try to propose solutions)
了解動物在人類社會中的位置 (understand the position of animals in human society)

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: Discusses the "Disappearing Taiwanese Mermaid" (Dugong), an aquatic mammal, which implicitly relates to the protection and restoration of water-related ecosystems (Target 6.6).
evidence: 消失的臺灣美人魚 (Disappearing Taiwanese Mermaid)

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Discusses "economic animals" and "performing animals," which relate to industries and could touch upon sustainable tourism and production patterns (Targets 8.4, 8.9).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The topic of "experimental animals" has a tenuous link to scientific research (Target 9.5), though the course focuses on ethics rather than innovation itself.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Discusses stray dogs in "modern society" from an "Institute of Building and Planning" perspective, linking to sustainable human settlement planning and management (Target 11.3).
evidence: 台灣現代社會如何面對狗的生物性 (how modern Taiwanese society faces the biological nature of dogs)
建築與城鄉研究所碩士論文 (Master's thesis, Institute of Building and Planning)

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Critiques human use of "economic animals" and "performing animals," directly addressing responsible consumption patterns and promoting awareness for sustainable lifestyles (Targets 12.2, 12.8).

Life Below Water.reason: The course explicitly discusses conservation of marine life, including "dolphins" and the "Dugong," directly aligning with protecting marine ecosystems and species (Targets 14.2, 14.5).
evidence: 海豚在香港：談野生海豚與圈養海豚的保育問題 (Dolphins in Hong Kong: conservation of wild and captive dolphins)
消失的臺灣美人魚 (Disappearing Taiwanese Mermaid)

Life on Land.reason: A core theme of the course, with explicit focus on "wildlife" conservation, including the "Taiwanese black bear" and "birdwatching," directly supporting targets to halt biodiversity loss (15.5, 15.7).
evidence: 黑熊手記：我與臺灣黑熊的故事 (Black Bear Notes: My Story with the Taiwanese Black Bear)

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course examines "institutional regulations" and "animal ethics," which relates to promoting and enforcing non-discriminatory laws and policies for sustainable development (Target 16.b).
evidence: 反動物實驗運動 (anti-animal experimentation movement)
